rationale_for_baseline_tier: Benign, painful plantar wart — routine treatment (paring/offloading + topical therapy) with a deep-infection safety-net; not urgent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Surrounding hot/red swelling, thick pus, fever, or continuous throbbing pain at rest
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the area becomes hot, red, swollen, oozes thick pus, or throbs even at rest, or you get a fever — see a doctor promptly.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Offloading, footwear and shower-hygiene advice to reduce pressure and reinfection.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a plantar wart. Have the thick skin pared and use a doughnut-shaped pad to take the pressure off (this stops the pain), plus daily salicylic-acid paint (or freezing) for weeks. Don't pumice it and wear thongs in public showers. Watch for heat/pus/fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP/podiatry in 2-4 weeks; prompt review if deep infection
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No urgent action needed unless deep-infection features.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document a plantar wart (deep infection/neuropathic ulcer screened negative), paring/offloading + salicylic acid ± cryotherapy, and hygiene advice.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: No deep-infection safety-net for a painful plantar lesion.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A deep abscess/infection is missed and progresses.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: No pain relief/offloading or definitive therapy, leaving the patient limping.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED referral for a benign painful wart.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot pare/probe remotely, but disrupted skin lines and black dots are characteristic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a plantar wart with offloading/paring + topical therapy and safety-net for deep infection.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Mislabelling as a corn, or over-triaging for abscess/foreign body.
